response.justification: Bogdan has a wide-ranging software background with genuine strengths in testing culture and some distributed systems exposure, but is fundamentally misaligned with the core requirements of this role. The position mandates 8 years of C++ experience as its primary technical qualifier; his resume shows only approximately 6 months of C/C++ work at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a passing mention in his bachelor thesis. His career stack is built almost entirely on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. There is no evidence of formal technical leadership over a cloud compute platform, no production cloud architecture ownership at scale, and no advanced degree. The C++ gap alone is disqualifying.
strengths: Consistent application of engineering best practices (TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD) across multiple employers and stacks
Real distributed-systems exposure at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic, writing low-latency services handling millions of HTTP requests per second
Staff-level seniority with demonstrated knowledge-sharing through workshops, conference talks, and open-source project leadership
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months at National Instruments in 2015) against the 8-year hard minimum requirement
No formal technical leadership role owning a cloud compute platform or equivalent large-scale infrastructure program
Primary technology stack (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) is entirely misaligned with the C++-centric platform mandate

key_factors.factor: No cloud platform or formal tech-lead ownership
Distributed systems and high-throughput service experience
